perturbation.original_text: Subject to paragraph 3 below, the term “Confidential Information” shall mean any and all materials or infor mation not in the public domain that either party receives or acquires from the other party in connection with any activities related thereto, and whether disclosed or made available prior to the date of thisagreement and whether in writing, electronically, orally, visually or otherwise and any notes,summaries or other materials in whatever medium or format (“Notes”) created by theReceiving Party and derived from such information or materials.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to paragraph 3 below, the term “Confidential Information” shall mean only materials and information specifically identified in writing as confidential at the time of disclosure. Any information disclosed orally, visually, or electronically, without contemporaneous written confirmation of its confidentiality, shall not be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change to the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1 introduces a contradiction. Originally, 'Confidential Information' included all materials 'whether in writing, electronically, orally, visually or otherwise'. The modified version now excludes orally, visually or electronically disclosed information unless it has contemporaneous written confirmation. This contradicts the original broad definition, especially concerning information shared before the agreement, which may not have such written confirmation. This contradiction creates uncertainty in enforcement, as it is unclear whether previous disclosures are covered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information. Subject to paragraph 3 below, the term “Confidential Information” shall mean only materials and information specifically identified in writing as confidential at the time of disclosure. Any information disclosed orally, visually, or electronically, without contemporaneous written confirmation of its confidentiality, shall not be considered Confidential Information. Confidential Informationincludes, without limitation, (i) software, utilities, solutions, designs, techniques, methods,methodologies, tools, processes, templates, data and any information related thereto,existing or contemplated products or services, specifications and plans, (ii) forecasts,business plans, strategies, and financial statements, records and information, (iii) customerlists or requirements, and (iv) other business or technical information or trade secrets. Theprovisions of this Agreement shall apply to any Confidential Information that a ReceivingParty receives or becomes privy to in connection with this Agreement, on or after theEffective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' to exclude orally, visually, or electronically disclosed information without contemporaneous written confirmation. The contradicted text contains the modified definition. The original text included all materials, 'whether in writing, electronically, orally, visually or otherwise' creating a direct conflict with the changed and contradicted text. Therefore, this creates a strong contradiction because the change limits the scope of 'Confidential Information' creating uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties anticipate that in connection with, any of the Parties may receive or become privy to the Confidential Infor mation of the other Parties. A Receiving Party agrees, acknowledges and undertakes to (i) hold the ConfidentialInformation of the Disclosing Party in strictest confidence, and subject to the terms ofthis Agreement, not to disclose Confidential Information to any third party unless it is aregulated investment body, solicitor, accountant or private investors known to the Party.Such information shall be shared freely.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties anticipate that in connection with, any of the Parties may receive or become privy to the Confidential Infor mation of the other Parties. A Receiving Party agrees, acknowledges and undertakes to (i) hold the ConfidentialInformation of the Disclosing Party in strictest confidence, and subject to the terms ofthis Agreement, to disclose Confidential Information to any third party without restriction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause about not disclosing confidential information to third parties in Section 2, and adding 'to disclose Confidential Information to any third party without restriction', this now directly contradicts the intention of the NDA, and creates an obligation to share the supposedly confidential information. The new insertion completely negates the initial purpose of this section. This creates uncertainty and a direct in-text contradiction, invalidating the purpose of the entire NDA, as the rest of the contract relies on this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure; Use Restrictions. The Parties anticipate that in connection with, any of the Parties may receive or become privy to the Confidential Infor mation of the other Parties. A Receiving Party agrees, acknowledges and undertakes to (i) hold the ConfidentialInformation of the Disclosing Party in strictest confidence, and subject to the terms ofthis Agreement, to disclose Confidential Information to any third party without restriction. Where the Party does not know the intended thirdparty then the Party shall ask for prior written consent of the Disclosing Party (which maybe given on such terms as the Disclosing Party considers appropriate), (ii) not to use theConfidential Information for any purpose (including but not limited to any competitive or Tabun Kitchen 151 Copse Hill, London, SW20 0SU2commercial purpose) other than in relation to the PR activities as contemplated under any other written agreement between the parties which references this Agreement, and (iii) protect such Confidential Information by using the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own highly Confidential Information of similar value and sensitivity, but not less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2 now states that confidential information should be disclosed to any third party without restriction. This directly contradicts the original intention of the NDA, as highlighted in the perturbation explanation, and therefore creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which expects the Receiving Party to hold the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in the strictest confidence. The change creates a clear and direct conflict within the document, making the NDA unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the provisions of this Agreement, the obligations and restrictions set forth herein regarding Confidential Information shall not apply to information that the Receiving Party can establish (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party, (ii) is lawfully received from a third party which is, to the reasonable knowledge and belief of the Receiving Party, not under any obligation of confidentiality for the benefit of the Disclosing Party, (iii) was either in the possession of or known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure without any limitation on use or disclosure for the benefit of the Receiving Party, or (iv) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without the use, r eference to or benefit of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the provisions of this Agreement, the obligations and restrictions set forth herein regarding Confidential Information shall only not apply to information that the Receiving Party can establish (i) is or becomes publicly available as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 3 severely restricts the exceptions to confidentiality. By omitting the other exceptions (lawfully received from a third party, already in possession, independently developed), it implies that even information received lawfully from another party or already known becomes confidential *unless* the public availability is due to a breach by the Receiving Party. The contradiction arises because it drastically narrows the scope of exceptions to confidentiality, conflicting with the intent of protecting only genuinely novel and secret information. The addition of 'only' also creates an implication that there are no other exception, which is not true, since previously this was one of the exceptions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exceptions. Notwithstanding the provisions of this Agreement, the obligations and restrictions set forth herein regarding Confidential Information shall only not apply to information that the Receiving Party can establish (i) is or becomes publicly available as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party. 4. Disclosures Required by Law or Regulation. A Receiving Party shall not be r estricted from disclosing Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party or any Notes as required pursuant to any law, regulation or judicial or governmental order, or request of a regulatory or self-regulatory entity having examination authority over Receiving Party or a member of its Group, provided that Receiving Party will disclose only such information as it believes is required to comply with the order or request and, in the case of a court order or subpoena and to the extent legally permitted, the Receiving Party shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party and shall if reasonably practicable, cooperate with the Disclosing Party, at the Disclosing Party’s expense, so that the Disclosing Party take appropriate remedies or other appropriate protection.
perturbation.justification: The changed text severely restricts the exceptions to confidentiality. It removes the clauses concerning information lawfully received, previously known, and independently developed. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, since the changed text now implies that unless the public availability is due to a breach by the Receiving Party, there are no other exceptions. The contradictory text expects that those other exceptions are in play, since they are at the beginning of the text. This change in the changed text alters the obligations and restrictions around confidentiality, creating a direct conflict with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: All materials and Confidential Infor mation shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. Except as expressly provided herein or under a separate written agreement between the parties that references this Agreement, either party shall not be obligated to grant, convey or transfer to the other any interest, license or other right, or under its Confidential Information or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark or other intellectual property right.
perturbation.changed_text: All materials and Confidential Infor mation shall become the joint property of both Parties from the moment of disclosure. Except as expressly provided herein or under a separate written agreement between the parties that references this Agreement, either party shall not be obligated to grant, convey or transfer to the other any interest, license or other right, or under its Confidential Information or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark or other intellectual property right.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 5 contradicts the fundamental principle of confidentiality and ownership. The original clause stated that Confidential Information remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. The modified version states it becomes joint property. This creates a direct conflict, as joint ownership implies the Receiving Party can use and disclose the information without restriction, directly opposing the core concept of an NDA. This directly affects the clauses about the return of Confidential Information in Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership; No License. All materials and Confidential Infor mation shall become the joint property of both Parties from the moment of disclosure. Except as expressly provided herein or under a separate written agreement between the parties that references this Agreement, either party shall not be obligated to grant, convey or transfer to the other any interest, license or other right, or under its Confidential Information or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark or other intellectual property right. 6. Return of Confidential Information. Upon the earlier of (i) the completion or (ii) the Disclosing Party’s written r equest, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party, in any form and including, all Notes, save to the extent that regulation, applicable rule, law or record retention policy requires retention of such material. Upon request, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that it has returned or destroyed all Confidential Information in accordance with this paragraph 6, disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that confidential information becomes the joint property of both parties, while the contradicted text (Section 6) states that the Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information. This creates a direct conflict, as joint ownership implies the Receiving Party can use and disclose the information without restriction, which directly opposes the requirement to return or destroy the information, indicating a clear contradiction. The original text stated that Confidential Information remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party, which is compatible with the return/destroy clause.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the earlier of (i) the completion or (ii) the Disclosing Party’s written r equest, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party, in any form and including, all Notes, save to the extent that regulation, applicable rule, law or record retention policy requires retention of such material. Upon request, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that it has returned or destroyed all Confidential Information in accordance with this paragraph 6, disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the earlier of (i) the completion or (ii) the Disclosing Party’s written r equest, the Receiving Party shall retain all Confidential Information disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party, in any form and including, all Notes, indefinitely, regardless of regulation, applicable rule, law or record retention policy. Upon request, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that it has retained all Confidential Information in accordance with this paragraph 6, disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: This modification to Section 6 introduces a direct contradiction. The original text mandates the return or destruction of Confidential Information. The changed text requires the Receiving Party to retain all Confidential Information indefinitely. This directly conflicts with the purpose of returning or destroying the information and contradicts the earlier clauses about maintaining confidentiality. This affects Section 8 (Duration) as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information. Upon the earlier of (i) the completion or (ii) the Disclosing Party’s written r equest, the Receiving Party shall retain all Confidential Information disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party, in any form and including, all Notes, indefinitely, regardless of regulation, applicable rule, law or record retention policy. Upon request, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that it has retained all Confidential Information in accordance with this paragraph 6, disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party. 7. No Warranty; Obligations. No r epresentations, warranties or other assurances are given by the either party with respect to the completeness or accuracy of any information or materials provided to the other party. Except for the obligations of the parties specifically set forth in this Agreement,
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information. The changed text requires the Receiving Party to retain all Confidential Information indefinitely, regardless of regulation, applicable rule, law or record retention policy. The contradicted text also states that the Receiving Party shall retain all Confidential Information indefinitely. This directly conflicts with the original requirement to return or destroy the information. The change in Section 6 creates a contradiction with the initial intention of returning the Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations undertaken by each party under this Agreement will be continuing and in particular shall survive termination of any discussions or negotiations between the parties regarding the funding activities following written notice from one party to the other party expressly terminating this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations undertaken by each party under this Agreement will cease immediately upon termination of any discussions or negotiations between the parties regarding the funding activities.
perturbation.explanation: This change to Section 8 directly contradicts the original intent that the obligations would survive the termination of discussions. It now states the obligations cease immediately upon termination, nullifying the long-term protection intended by the NDA. This contradicts the confidentiality clauses, since the need for the NDA arises, not from just negotiations, but also beyond. Therefore, that clause is invalidated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duration. The obligations undertaken by each party under this Agreement will cease immediately upon termination of any discussions or negotiations between the parties regarding the funding activities following written notice from one party to the other party expressly terminating this Agreement. 9. Governing Law; Remedies. This Agr eement, including, without limitation, the performance and enforceability hereof, shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England & Wales. Each Receiving Party acknowledges that a violation of this Agreement by the Receiving Party may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party and that the Disclosing Party may have no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, each party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights and Tabun Kitchen 151 Copse Hill, London, SW20 0SU3 remedies it may have, at law, in equity or otherwise, to seek injunctive relief or similar such orders in any court of competent jurisdiction directing or prohibiting certain acts which may be necessary to protect the Confidential Information and the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that obligations cease immediately upon termination of discussions, directly contradicting the contradicted text (which is identical to the changed text) but which originally stated the opposite in the original text. The original text indicated that obligations would survive termination, ensuring continued confidentiality. By changing this, the new text creates a direct conflict within the document regarding the duration of obligations, particularly concerning confidentiality after discussions end, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation. This fits the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
